description_long: This object consists of three large, dark wooden crates arranged in a pyramid formation, with two crates at the base and one stacked on top. Each crate is constructed from vertical wooden planks, reinforced at the edges with thicker wooden beams and secured with visible metal bolts at the corners. The wood has a weathered, slightly rough texture, suggesting it is designed for durability in outdoor or industrial urban environments. The crates are closed on all visible sides, with no handles, hinges, or openings apparent, giving them a solid, boxy appearance. The coloration is a deep, muted brownish-gray, consistent across all surfaces, and the construction appears robust enough to support significant weight. This arrangement is typical for storage or makeshift shelving in garages, warehouses, or urban back alleys.
description: A set of three large, dark wooden crates stacked in a pyramid, featuring reinforced corners and a weathered, industrial look. The crates are solid, closed, and suitable for storage or shelving in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the vertical planks and reinforced corners of the two stacked crates, with the top crate centered above the bottom one.
description_view_1: Angled view displaying all three crates in a pyramid arrangement, with two crates at the base and one on top, highlighting the uniform plank construction.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the vertical planks and reinforced corners of the two stacked crates, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side of view 1, again showing the pyramid arrangement and the solid, closed faces of the crates.
